skill.name: css-development-create-component
skill.description: Guides creation of reusable CSS components with semantic class names, Tailwind @apply composition, dark mode variants, documentation, and tests.
skill.summary: Create consistent CSS components that match existing project patterns.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are almost entirely Markdown code spans, CSS examples, and developer workflow snippets. The remaining risk is low because the skill asks the agent to edit local CSS and test files and run ordinary project tests, with no evidence of malicious intent, credential access, network exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The repeated external command hits on lines such as 12, 17, and 31 are inline Markdown references to CSS syntax, class names, and skill names. They are not executable code and do not create a command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown documentation around Tailwind, class naming, and dark mode. No shell API, script file, or dynamic execution mechanism exists in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Documented Test Commands Require Normal User Oversight
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples to run npm test or vitest against project test files. This is a legitimate coding workflow, but it can execute project-local test scripts, so marketplace users should expect local command execution when applying the workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and easy to verify. They run standard local test tooling and do not include remote downloads, shell interpolation, or hidden payloads.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Reads Appear Only in Test Examples
low_findings.description: The synchronous file operation findings point to a sample Vitest test that reads styles/components.css. This is not runtime skill behavior and does not access sensitive paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The readFileSync usage is embedded in a documented test snippet. The target path is a project CSS file, not user secrets or system files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography hits do not correspond to crypto APIs, hashing functions, encryption routines, or password handling. The cited lines contain skill metadata, prose, naming examples, or test framework imports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation in the cited context or elsewhere in SKILL.md. The static matcher appears to have triggered on unrelated substrings in Markdown content.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance hit references text about adding semantic CSS classes. No evidence found of network scanning, host discovery, socket use, HTTP requests, or external endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a plain CSS workflow bullet. The skill contains no network instructions or network-capable code files.

content.value_statement: New UI components often drift from established CSS patterns and miss dark mode or tests. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a repeatable component workflow.
actual_capabilities: Surveys existing component CSS before adding new classes.
Checks whether existing classes can be composed instead of creating more CSS.
Guides semantic class naming for buttons, cards, forms, layouts, and states.
Provides a Tailwind @apply structure with dark mode and interaction states.
Recommends static CSS tests and framework rendering tests.
Prompts documentation through usage comments and markup examples.
limitations: It assumes the project uses Tailwind or compatible utility classes.
It does not generate visual screenshots or design mockups by itself.
It requires the agent to inspect project files before choosing exact names.
It provides examples, not a complete design system architecture.

use_cases.title: Add a Reusable Button Variant
use_cases.description: Create a semantic button class that matches existing naming, supports dark mode, and includes focused tests.

use_cases.title: Standardize Card Styling
use_cases.description: Turn repeated card markup into a documented component pattern without duplicating utility classes everywhere.

use_cases.title: Review Component CSS Before Merge
use_cases.description: Check that new CSS follows project conventions, has usage documentation, and includes relevant test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small UI element.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new primary button component style. Follow the css-development-create-component workflow and reuse existing classes if possible.

prompt_templates.title: Add Dark Mode Support
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a styled component with theme coverage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a semantic alert component class with light and dark mode states. Include usage documentation and a static CSS test.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Repeated Markup
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to reduce duplicated utility classes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these repeated card patterns and decide whether composition is enough. If not, create a semantic card class and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Design System Component Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before merging a component library change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the proposed component CSS for naming consistency, Tailwind composition, accessibility states, dark mode coverage, and test gaps.

output_examples.input: Create a success badge style for status labels.
output: Confirms whether an existing badge class can be reused.
Recommends a semantic class name such as badge-success.
Describes light mode, dark mode, spacing, and typography requirements.
Lists the static CSS checks needed for the new class.

output_examples.input: Add a reusable empty state card.
output: Classifies the component as a molecule because it combines content, spacing, and actions.
Suggests documentation for React and plain HTML usage.
Identifies rendering tests that verify the semantic class is applied.

output_examples.input: Review a new navigation component before merge.
output: Checks whether names match existing project conventions.
Flags missing focus, active, or dark mode states.
Summarizes required test and documentation updates.

best_practices: Read existing component CSS before naming or adding a new class.
Prefer composition when existing semantic classes already solve the need.
Add tests and usage documentation in the same change as the component CSS.
anti_patterns: Do not create visual names such as btn-blue when a semantic name is clearer.
Do not add a new class when existing classes compose cleanly.
Do not ship interactive styles without hover, focus, disabled, and dark mode states where relevant.

faq.question: Does this skill only work with Tailwind?
faq.answer: It is written for Tailwind @apply workflows, but the naming and testing guidance can inform other CSS systems.

faq.question: Will it create components automatically?
faq.answer: It guides the agent through component creation. The agent still needs project context and file access.

faq.question: Does it support dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow explicitly asks for dark mode variants when colors or backgrounds are involved.

faq.question: What tests does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends static CSS tests for class existence and rendering tests for framework component class application.

faq.question: Can it prevent duplicate CSS?
faq.answer: It reduces duplication by checking whether existing classes can be composed before adding a new class.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found low risk. It contains normal local coding workflow instructions and no evidence of malicious behavior.
